reportBuilder.allReportsDescription: Manage all created reports, update them, and track sharing status.
reportBuilder.firstTimeHintDescription: Pick a data source and validate it first. Once the schema is ready, field selection and visuals become available.
reportBuilder.stepProgressDatasetDone: Data source is connected and ready for preview.
reportBuilder.stepProgressDatasetPending: Select a data source (view or function) and validate it.
reportBuilder.stepProgressIdentityPending: Give the report a clear, recognizable name.
reportBuilder.stepProgressFieldsPending: Pick the fields to show and, if needed, axis/metric.

starterKitDescriptions.operations: A table-first start that fits daily follow-up work.
starterKitDescriptions.performance: A fast, result-focused start with a large KPI card.

reportBuilder.basicBigChoiceTitle: What kind of report do you want?
reportBuilder.basicBigChoiceDescription: Choose one of the three options below and the system will start you with the right setup.
reportBuilder.basicChoiceExecutive: I want a summary screen for management.
reportBuilder.basicChoiceOperations: I want a table-first screen for daily follow-up.
reportBuilder.basicChoicePerformance: I want a KPI-led screen focused on targets.
reportBuilder.basicSetupDescription: Build your report here with a few simple choices, without dealing with technical terms.
reportBuilder.basicChooseVisualDescription: Choose a table, chart, or KPI view. The system helps with the rest.
reportBuilder.basicChooseGroupByDescription: Pick the field that should split the rows or the chart.
reportBuilder.basicChooseDateGroupingTitle: How should the date be grouped?
reportBuilder.basicChooseMetricDescription: Choose the main numeric field to total or calculate.
reportBuilder.basicChooseAggregationDescription: Choose whether you want total, count, or average.
reportBuilder.basicChooseBreakdownTitle: 5. Add an extra breakdown if you want
reportBuilder.basicChooseBreakdownDescription: You can color or split the chart by a second field for more detail.
reportBuilder.basicSummaryDescription: Your report will currently be shown with this logic.
reportBuilder.simpleTableDescription: First choose the view. Then pick the fields from that view, rename the headers, and save. In basic mode that is all you need.
reportBuilder.simpleAvailableFieldsDescription: Pick the fields you want to see in the report. They are added to the table immediately.
reportBuilder.simpleSelectedFieldsDescription: Rename the headers here and arrange the field order.
reportBuilder.simpleNoFieldsSelectedDescription: When you pick fields from the list, the table is created automatically.
reportBuilder.advancedGridDescription: The main face of advanced mode is a powerful table workspace. Add charts only if you need them later.
reportBuilder.advancedGridPrimaryDescription: Start with a strong table. Place fields, headers, and ordering first. Add a chart only if it helps afterwards.
reportBuilder.advancedGridPrimaryHelper: This is the safest starting point for dense operational datasets.
reportBuilder.advancedGridColumnsDescription: Choose which fields appear in the table, reorder them, and adjust the visible headers.
reportBuilder.advancedGridFieldsDescription: Pick fields from the view and add them to the grid.
reportBuilder.optionalVisualsDescription: Once the grid is ready, you can optionally add a chart or KPI view for the same data.
reportBuilder.simpleBuilderChecklistDescription: In basic mode the dataset, field selection, and report name are enough.
reportBuilder.simpleChecklistFieldsPending: You should select at least one field.
reportBuilder.simpleChecklistNamePending: Enter the report name before saving.
reportBuilder.simpleTopbarDescription: Connect and validate the view here. Then you can move on to field selection.
reportBuilder.simpleFinalStepDescription: If everything looks right, pressing save is enough.
reportBuilder.simpleSaveHintDescription: When you click the save button above, the report is created and you are taken straight to the report screen.
reportBuilder.saveSuccessCreatedDescription: The report has been created. You can review the result here or continue editing it.
reportBuilder.saveSuccessUpdatedDescription: The report has been updated. You can review the result here or continue editing it.
reportBuilder.simpleSummaryDescription: In basic mode the report is saved as a plain table.

reportBuilder.dashboardFiltersEmptyDescription: When you add a field, the shared filter for the whole report will appear here.

fieldGlossary.dimension: These fields are used for grouping and breakdowns such as user, customer, or status.
fieldGlossary.metric: These fields are summed, counted, or averaged. The main number in the report comes from them.
fieldGlossary.date: These fields are best for trend and period-based analysis.
fieldGlossary.customer: Useful for customer-based comparisons and commercial analysis.
fieldGlossary.user: Useful for user, sales rep, or owner performance analysis.
fieldGlossary.status: Useful for distributions such as waiting, completed, or cancelled.
fieldGlossary.amount: Useful for financial measures such as amount, total, or price.

repairDescriptions.tooComplexPie: When there are too many series or more than one value field, a pie chart becomes hard to read. A simple bar chart is usually a better fit.
repairDescriptions.tooManySeries: This view is currently producing {{count}} separate series. Most users will struggle to read that many lines at once.
repairDescriptions.nonDateLine: Line charts make the most sense with a date or period axis. A bar chart will be easier to read here.
repairDescriptions.dateBar: For single-series date-based data, a line trend usually tells the story more clearly.

repairActions.switchBar: Switch to a better bar chart

reportBuilder.guardrailTitle: This view may be hard for users to read
reportBuilder.guardrailTooManySeries: This widget is about to render {{count}} separate series. When there are many users or many breakdowns, the chart becomes noisy.

chartTypeDisabledReasons.lineNeedsDate: Pick a date axis for line charts.
chartTypeDisabledReasons.pieSingleValue: Use a single value field for pie and donut charts.
chartTypeDisabledReasons.matrixNeedsBreakdown: Matrix views need both an axis and a breakdown field.

aggregationRecommendations.countIdentifiers: Identifier, code, and status fields usually read better as counts.
aggregationRecommendations.countRecords: This field is usually easier to understand as a record count.
aggregationRecommendations.averageRates: Rate and percentage fields are usually clearer as averages.
aggregationRecommendations.sumTotals: Amount, quantity, and total fields are usually best summed.

chartTypeDescriptions.table: Best for reviewing detailed row-level data directly.
chartTypeDescriptions.bar: Best for comparing magnitudes across categories.
chartTypeDescriptions.stackedBar: Best for comparing segment contribution inside totals.
chartTypeDescriptions.line: Best for tracking movement and trends over time.
chartTypeDescriptions.pie: Best for showing ratio distribution for a single metric.
chartTypeDescriptions.donut: Best for an executive-friendly ratio view with a cleaner card feel.
chartTypeDescriptions.kpi: Best for emphasizing one standout metric and its change.
chartTypeDescriptions.matrix: Best for pivot-style summaries with row and column breakdowns.

customer360.title: Customer 360
customer360.subtitle: Customer overview and activity

analytics.error: Analytics data could not be loaded

customer360.error: An error occurred while loading the overview

INCREASE_ACTIVITY_CADENCE.title: Increase activity cadence
INCREASE_ACTIVITY_CADENCE.reason: Only 2 activities in the last 7 days.

explain.revenueQualityTitle: Revenue quality scores are rule-based and calculated from the last 12 months of order/quotation/activity data, covering cohort, retention, RFM, LTV, churn, upsell and payment metrics.
explain.churnRisk: Churn Risk is calculated between 0-100 using last order recency, last activity recency, and 12-month order frequency signals. Higher score means higher loss risk.
explain.upsellPropensity: Upsell Propensity is scored 0-100 from order count, quotation-to-order conversion, and average order amount signals. Higher score indicates stronger upsell opportunity.
explain.paymentBehavior: Payment Behavior is currently a proxy score (order continuity, quotation rejection rate, last-order recency) instead of direct ERP collection history, calculated 0-100.
explain.rfmSegment: RFM Segment assigns Champions/Loyal/AtRisk/Potential based on recency, frequency, and monetary rules.
explain.cohortRetentionTitle: Cohort Retention uses first touch/order month as cohort; if there is an order in following months, it counts as retained and produces monthly retention percentage.
explain.recommendedActionsTitle: Recommended Actions are priority follow-up suggestions produced by the rules engine (e.g. no activity for a long time, there is an open offer, churn is high). Each action can be tracked with justification + rule code.
explain.executeAction: Execute converts this recommendation into an activity/task: backend calls `POST /api/customer360/{id}/actions/execute` and creates a new Activity record.
explain.noCohortData: Cohort data is not available yet due to insufficient order history.
explain.modelNote: Note: These scores are deterministic v1 and can be improved later with ERP collection data, quota/forecast signals, and ML models.

salesman360.title: Sales Representative 360
salesman360.subtitle: Sales representative overview
salesman360.error: An error occurred while loading the overview

analytics.error: Analytics data could not be loaded

INCREASE_ACTIVITY_CADENCE.title: Increase activity cadence
INCREASE_ACTIVITY_CADENCE.reason: Only {{count}} activities in the last 7 days.

explain.revenueQualityTitle: Scores are rule-based, calculated from the sales rep's last 12 months portfolio/order/activity performance, covering cohort, retention, RFM, LTV, churn, upsell and payment metrics.
explain.churnRisk: Portfolio Churn Risk is scored 0-100 based on active customers versus customers becoming inactive in the last 90 days.
explain.upsellPropensity: Upsell Propensity is scored 0-100 using order intensity, won quotations, and activity cadence signals.
explain.paymentBehavior: Payment Behavior currently uses proxy metrics; it becomes more accurate once direct ERP collection history is integrated.
explain.rfmSegment: RFM Segment classifies the portfolio by recency/frequency/monetary behavior.
explain.cohortRetentionTitle: Cohort Retention cohorts customers by first order month within the rep portfolio and shows monthly retention rates.
explain.recommendedActionsTitle: Recommended Actions recommend priority actions such as pipeline cleaning, activity increase and churn intervention. Each action can be tracked with justification + rule code.
explain.executeAction: Execute converts the recommendation into an activity/task: backend calls `POST /api/salesmen360/{userId}/actions/execute`.
explain.noCohortData: Cohort data is not available yet due to insufficient order history.
explain.modelNote: Note: Current scores are deterministic v1 and can be strengthened with quota/forecast/ML.

catalogStockPicker.title: Select Stock From Catalog
catalogStockPicker.description: Browse the catalog by category, then pick stock from the linked product list.
catalogStockPicker.hierarchyBlueprintDescription: Keep the same structure in the catalog: main category, subcategory, brand, series, and finally products.
catalogStockPicker.hierarchyExampleValue: Phones > Feature Phones > iPhone > 17 Series > Products

catalogStockPicker.rightPanelLeafHint: Pick the stock from the cards below. After selection it is applied exactly like the current magnifier flow.

Fields.RestrictCustomersBySalesRepMatch: Restrict customers by sales rep match

Descriptions.RestrictCustomersBySalesRepMatch: When enabled, customer lists and pickers are limited by the selected user's matched sales representative codes.
Descriptions.DecimalPlacesRange: Decimal places must be between 0 and 6.
Descriptions.VatVisibility: When enabled, the VAT rate input is hidden on the related demand, quotation, or order line forms. Calculation data is preserved.
Descriptions.VatReadonly: When enabled, the VAT rate remains visible on the related demand, quotation, or order lines but cannot be changed by the user.
Descriptions.CatalogLabels: Leave empty to keep the default Netsis field names such as Group Code, Code1, and Code2. Maximum 50 characters.

Quotation.6: Create SalesDesk order and Netsis order

Order.4: Create Netsis order and sales invoice

LineFormSection.Title: Line form (Profile / Rebar / Screw)
LineFormSection.Description: These options are stored only in this browser, not on the server. Click Save below to apply them.
LineFormSection.ShowBlockHint: When off, the three fields are hidden on line forms; saving a line sends those values empty.

DemandActionLabels.5: Open demand in Netsis and create quotation

QuotationActionLabels.5: Open quotation in Netsis and create order
QuotationActionLabels.6: Open order record in SalesDesk and create order in Netsis

OrderActionLabels.4: Open order and sales invoice in Netsis

DemandActionTooltips.1: When all approval steps are completed, the demand will remain in approved status only.
DemandActionTooltips.2: When all approval steps are completed, the system will create a demand record in Netsis.
DemandActionTooltips.3: When all approval steps are completed, a quotation record will be created in CRM.
DemandActionTooltips.4: When all approval steps are completed, the system will create a demand record in Netsis.
DemandActionTooltips.5: When all approval steps are completed, a demand will be opened in Netsis and a quotation will be created in CRM.

QuotationActionTooltips.1: When all approval steps are completed, the quotation will remain in approved status only.
QuotationActionTooltips.2: When all approval steps are completed, the system will create a quotation record in Netsis.
QuotationActionTooltips.3: When all approval steps are completed, an order record will be created in CRM.
QuotationActionTooltips.4: When all approval steps are completed, the system will create an order record in Netsis.
QuotationActionTooltips.5: When all approval steps are completed, a quotation will be opened in Netsis and an order will be created in CRM.
QuotationActionTooltips.6: When all approval steps are completed, an order will be created in SalesDesk and an order record will be opened in Netsis.

OrderActionTooltips.1: When all approval steps are completed, the order will remain in approved status only.
OrderActionTooltips.2: When all approval steps are completed, the system will create an order record in Netsis.
OrderActionTooltips.3: When all approval steps are completed, the system will create a sales invoice in Netsis.
OrderActionTooltips.4: When all approval steps are completed, an order and sales invoice will be created in Netsis.
